documentation.readme: Quickly identify common Windows vulnerabilities and configuration issues that are not necessarily covered by public security standards, and collect useful information for exploitation and post-exploitation tasks.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zynon/LdapRelayScan (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zodiacon/HookHunter (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zmap/zmap (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the post_exploitation category. Fits when the engagement needs: Quickly identify common Windows vulnerabilities and configuration issues that are not necessarily covered by public security standards, and collect useful information for exploitation and post-exploitation tasks.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Quickly identify common Windows vulnerabilities and configuration issues that are not necessarily covered by public security standards, and collect useful information for exploitation and post-exploitation tasks.
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `PrivescCheck` from its upstream repository, then run `PrivescCheck --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags. Quickly identify common Windows vulnerabilities and configuration issues that are not necessarily covered by public security standards, and collect useful information for exploitation and post-exploitation tasks.

name: PrivescCheck
summary: Quickly identify common Windows vulnerabilities and configuration issues that are not necessarily covered by public security standards, and collect useful information for exploitation and post-exploitation tasks.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need post_exploitation, use PrivescCheck because Quickly identify common Windows vulnerabilities and configuration issues that are not necessarily covered by public security standards, and collect useful infor.
When chaining post_exploitation tools, pair PrivescCheck with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `PrivescCheck --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
